summary: Auth migration to httpOnly cookie session verified end-to-end. Backend: /api/auth/register and /api/auth/login return no 'token' field in body and set Set-Cookie: session_token HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None. /api/auth/me returns 200 with cookie, 401 without. /api/auth/logout clears cookie and subsequent /api/auth/me returns 401. All auth-gated endpoints (/api/palm/reports, /api/palm/reports/{id}, /api/referral/me, /api/subscription/status, /api/admin/stats) return 200 using only the cookie. Frontend: login on /auth with demo credentials redirects to /dashboard, localStorage has no 'pm_token' or 'session_token', document.cookie does not expose session_token (confirming HttpOnly), dashboard reload preserves session, report /report/rpt_efc49a2be09c fully loads, logout clears session and protected routes redirect to /auth.
passed: POST /api/auth/login: 200, Set-Cookie session_token HttpOnly Secure SameSite=None, body has {user:{...}} and NO 'token' field
POST /api/auth/register (new email): 200, Set-Cookie session_token present, body has NO 'token' field
GET /api/auth/me with cookie: 200 returns user object
POST /api/auth/logout: 200, Set-Cookie session_token="" Max-Age=0; subsequent /api/auth/me returns 401
localStorage contains NO pm_token or session_token after login (only rzp/posthog keys)
document.cookie does NOT contain session_token (HttpOnly enforced)
Dashboard reload persists user session via cookie
Logout button clears session; visiting /dashboard redirects to /auth
notes: Post-logout 401s in console are expected (AuthContext /api/auth/me check on protected route before redirect). Razorpay preload warnings are pre-existing and unrelated.
